summary: Duplicate sidecarFailure JSX block in safe-mode.tsx — renders twice
detail: The {diag?.sidecarFailure && (...)} block appears twice in safe-mode.tsx (around lines 75-84 and 87-102), likely a merge artifact from parallel lane work. Each block renders the same "Sidecar Startup Failure" panel — the second copy must be removed.

summary: writePhase() calls use incorrect phase names in sidecar.ts phases 2-6
detail: Phases 2-6 in the per-phase try/catch blocks reuse incorrect writePhase() names. Phase 2 (loopback-proxy) uses "sidecar.env.prepare" instead of a distinct name. Phase 3 (system-certs) and Phase 4 (env-proxy) also reuse "sidecar.env.prepare". Phase 6 (log-init) reuses "sidecar.config.load" from Phase 5. This degrades diagnostic clarity in sidecar-startup.jsonl.

summary: SidecarStartupFailure type missing errorCode field present in JSON output
detail: The sidecar.ts fail() function writes an errorCode field (from classifyError()) into sidecar-boot-failure.json, but the SidecarStartupFailure type in types.ts does not include errorCode. This means the index.ts getSafeModeDiagnostics handler parses the JSON into an object that TypeScript types as SidecarStartupFailure | null, but the errorCode property is untyped. Either add errorCode?: string to the type, or stop writing errorCode to the JSON.
